journal_entry: Alhamdulillah I finally got the job offer I prayed so hard for. After months of interviews and rejections, Allah opened this door for me. I want to thank Him properly and not forget Him now that things are going well.
My daughter was born yesterday and I cannot describe this joy. I held her and cried and kept saying Alhamdulillah. I want to raise her on deen from day one.
I passed my final exam with distinction! I was so scared I would fail. I prostrated in shukr right there in my room. Allah is so Merciful.
My brother and I spoke for the first time in three years today. I had been making dua for this for so long. We both cried and forgave each other.
The doctors said my scans are clear. After two years of treatment and not knowing if I would live, I am free. I do not even know how to thank Allah enough.
My mother passed away last week. I feel empty. I keep reaching for my phone to call her and then remembering. The house is so quiet.
My best friend since childhood stopped talking to me after my wedding. She was cold and distant and now she blocked me. I keep crying.
I lost the baby at 14 weeks. Everyone tells me I am young and it will happen again but I was already a mother in my heart. I do not know how to keep going.
I moved abroad for studies and it has been six months. I miss my family so much it physically hurts. I cry after every phone call home.
My marriage ended this month. Ten years gone. I did not want this but I had no choice. I feel like my whole life collapsed.
I have my final exam tomorrow and I have not slept in two days. My heart is racing and I keep thinking I am going to fail and disappoint my parents who sacrificed everything for me.
I have a big interview in the morning. I keep rehearsing answers and none feel good enough. What if they see through me? What if I freeze?
Results come back Monday. I cannot eat, I cannot focus. Every time my phone rings I jump. I keep imagining the worst news.
My visa decision is pending and I have been refreshing the portal every hour for three weeks. If it is rejected my whole life plan collapses.
My son is being bullied at school and I feel helpless. I lie awake at night worrying about him. I do not know how to protect him from the world.
My business partner of eight years stole from me. He lied to my face while emptying accounts. I want to destroy him. I cannot pray because I am shaking with rage.
I got passed over for the promotion again and it went to someone who did half my work. My boss lied about the reasons. I am furious.
My uncle spread rumors about my wife to the whole family. Nobody defended her. I do not want to see any of them ever again.
I keep seeing videos of what is happening to Muslims around the world and I cannot sleep. I am angry at everyone who stays silent. I feel powerless and furious.
My husband lied about something small but it was a lie. I feel like I cannot trust him now and I am angrier than I should be.
There is a marriage proposal and I do not know what to do. On paper he is good but something in my heart hesitates. I have prayed istikhara but I still cannot decide.
I have two job offers. One pays more, the other aligns with my values. I keep flipping between them and losing sleep.
Why did Allah allow this to happen to me? I know I should trust the plan but I genuinely do not understand. I feel lost in the confusion.
I do not know if I am raising my kids right. Every scholar says something different. I second-guess every decision I make.
I do not know what to do with my life. Medicine? Business? My parents want one thing, my heart wants another, and I cannot tell which is right.
Alhamdulillah a year ago I was in the ICU. Today I walked 5 kilometers. I never thought I would feel my legs again. Every breath is gratitude.
I prayed for this child for eight years. Today he called me mama for the first time. I cannot stop thanking Allah.
My father and I had not spoken in five years. Today he called me and we cried on the phone. Allah softened both our hearts.
The car accident should have killed me. The other driver walked away too. I keep thinking how Allah protected us both. I want to be a better person.
I sat in the sun this morning with tea and watched the birds. At my age every simple day feels like a huge blessing. Alhamdulillah for the small things.
I am the only Muslim in my program and I eat every meal alone. I pray alone, I break my fast alone. Weekends are the worst. I feel invisible.
Since I became Muslim my old friends stopped inviting me and I have not found new ones. I feel stuck between two worlds and belong to neither.
My wife passed away two years ago and my children are busy with their lives. The house is silent. I talk to the walls now.
I moved to a new city for work and I do not know a single person. I spent Eid alone in my apartment scrolling through photos from home.
I am married but I feel completely alone. He is there but he is not there. I have never felt this lonely in my life.
I have three major deliverables due this week and my team is short-staffed. I have not slept more than four hours in five days. I feel like I am drowning.
The bills are piling up and I do not know how I will pay rent this month. I keep calculating numbers and they never add up. My chest is constantly tight.
I am caring for my sick mother, raising three kids, and trying to work. I have not had a single hour to myself in months. I am going to break.
Final exams start Monday and I have six subjects I barely know. My hands shake when I try to study. I keep crying in the library.
Wedding planning is destroying me. Both families are fighting over every detail and I am in the middle trying to keep the peace. I cry every night.
I am terrified about the world my children will grow up in. The news, the fitnah, everything. I pray over them every night and still the fear does not leave.
The doctor used the word cancer today. I do not remember anything after that. I am scared of dying but more scared of suffering first.
I am afraid I will die before I have repented properly. My sins feel mountains high and my deeds feel like nothing. Every night I fear I will not wake up.
My ex keeps showing up at my work. I am scared for my safety and for my children. The restraining order feels like paper.
I graduated six months ago and I have no job. I am running out of savings. I am scared I will end up a failure and disappoint everyone.
Before Islam I did things I cannot forgive myself for. I know Allah forgives but I cannot let go of the shame. It eats me every day.
I have been missing Fajr for weeks. I keep promising myself I will pray and then I sleep through it. I feel like a fraud.
I raised my voice at my mother last week and she cried. I cannot stop replaying it. She is old and I treated her badly over nothing.
I have been working so much I barely see my kids. My son asked me today if I still love him. I am failing as a mother.
I said something cruel to my wife last night. I saw her face break and I did not apologize properly. I feel sick about myself.
Another rejection today. Number 47. I do not know why I keep trying. Nothing I do is working. I feel like giving up on life plans entirely.
I cannot see a future. I wake up and wish I had not woken up. Not wanting to die, just not wanting to be here. Everything feels gray.
I am 40 and I have achieved nothing meaningful. My peers are successful, married, happy. I feel stuck in a life I cannot change.
I have been making the same dua for years. Still nothing. I am starting to feel like Allah is not listening. I am exhausted of asking.
Every time I try to rebuild my life something collapses again. I am tired of starting over. I do not see the point in trying anymore.
Work deadlines, three kids, a sick parent, and trying to pray five times a day. I feel pulled in every direction and failing at all of them.
Some days I am fine. Other days everything at once hits me and I cannot breathe. I do not know how to handle these waves.
I am trying to study, pray Tahajjud, memorize Quran, work part-time, and be a good daughter. Every day I collapse and feel like a failure.
There is so much to learn as a new Muslim. Every scholar says something different about every topic. I am drowning in information.
My team depends on me, my parents depend on me, my wife depends on me. I have no space to be weak. I am about to break.
My marriage proposal was turned down for the third time. She said she liked me but her family wanted someone else. I feel worthless.
My friend group went on a trip without inviting me. I saw the photos on Instagram. They acted normal at class today. I feel invisible.
The company I really wanted rejected me after three rounds. They hired someone with less experience. I am questioning my entire worth.
My own family excluded me from my cousin's wedding. No explanation. Just not on the list. I have never felt so cast aside.
I tried to share Islam with my parents gently and they laughed at me and told me I was brainwashed. They have not called in weeks.
I gave a presentation to senior management and completely blanked on the main slide. They were silent for ten seconds. I want to disappear.
Someone shared a private message of mine in a group chat. Everyone saw it. I cannot show my face at university now.
At the iftar I said something really stupid in front of everyone. They all laughed awkwardly. I have been replaying it for three days.
Someone at the masjid found out about my past and hinted at it in front of others. My face was burning. I do not want to go back.
At the family gathering everyone asked about my job and I had to say I still have nothing. My cousin's pity was worse than the question.
I have money, a family, a career. Every box ticked. And yet I feel empty. I do not know what my purpose is anymore. Is this all there is?
I am having thoughts about Islam I never had before. I do not know if I still believe the way I used to. I am scared and I feel lost.
My family does not recognize me, the Muslim community sees me as an outsider. I do not know who I am or where I belong anymore.
I spent five years studying something I now hate. I do not know what I want to do with my life. Every path feels wrong.
I defined myself as a wife for ten years. Now I am not. I do not know who I am without him. I feel completely lost.
help

Today started badly because my alarm did not go off and I missed fajr again which I have been doing for weeks and I feel terrible about it, then on the way to work traffic was awful and a car almost hit me and I yelled at the driver which was wrong of me, at work my boss criticized me in front of the whole team over a mistake that was not even my fault and I could feel my face burning and I almost cried but I held it in, at lunch my mother called and we argued about my future marriage prospects again, in the afternoon I found out a coworker has been taking credit for my work for months and our shared manager just shrugged when I brought it up, coming home the bus was packed and someone stepped on my foot and did not apologize, at dinner my father complained about the food and my mother cried quietly, after isha I sat on the prayer mat and could not even form a coherent dua because there was too much going on inside me and I just cried instead, and now it is midnight and I cannot sleep and I keep replaying every single moment of this terrible day and I feel like I am drowning in everything and I do not know how to even begin sorting through all of this, I feel like I am going to break into pieces and nobody would notice, everything at once is too much, I used to be able to handle life but something changed and I cannot anymore, I do not know if I am strong enough for whatever Allah is testing me with.
I am jealous of my friend's life.
My mother died last week and I cannot stop crying. The house feels empty without her voice.
I keep making dua but يا رب I feel like nothing is changing. The silence is heavy.
Bohot pareshan hoon, kuch samajh nahi aa raha, kaam bhi nahi ho raha aur ghar mein bhi tension hai.
Saya rasa sangat sedih hari ini, saya tidak tahu kenapa. Semua terasa berat.
Oh great, another amazing day of failure. Everything is just wonderful. I love when nothing works.
My grandfather died last night and the last time we spoke I was angry and hung up on him. I feel grief and guilt and rage all at once.
I want to kill myself. I cannot do this anymore.
I just want to end it all. There is no point anymore.
I have been thinking about suicide more and more every day.
Life is not worth living. I feel nothing anymore.
I want to die. I am tired of waking up every morning.
I have been thinking about how to hurt myself. I do not know who to tell.
There is no point living like this anymore.
Everyone would be better off dead than stuck with me.
I can't go on. Every day is worse than the last.
I want to give up on life. I am done trying.
I have been doing self harm again and I cannot stop.
I keep thinking about ending my life lately.
I have been thinking about how to take my life.
I was so embarrassed I was dying of embarrassment, I wanted to melt into the floor.
This job is killing me, the hours are brutal and I have no time for anything.
I could die of happiness right now. Alhamdulillah for everything.
